name: Subclinical Hypothyroidism
reasoning: Elevated TSH with potentially low Free T4 suggests underactive thyroid function. Subclinical hypothyroidism may progress to overt hypothyroidism.
recommendation: Full thyroid panel (Free T4, Free T3, anti-TPO). Repeat TSH in 6-8 weeks if borderline.

name: Hyperuricemia / Gout Risk
reasoning: Elevated uric acid increases risk of gout and urate nephropathy. Co-existing renal impairment may reduce uric acid excretion.
recommendation: Dietary purine restriction, hydration. Consider 24-hour urine uric acid if recurrent. Evaluate for allopurinol therapy.

name: Pre-diabetes Detection
reasoning: HbA1c in the 5.7-6.4% range indicates pre-diabetes. Combined with impaired fasting glucose, risk of progression to type 2 diabetes is significant.
recommendation: Lifestyle modification: weight management, 150 min/week moderate exercise, reduced refined carbohydrates. Repeat HbA1c in 3-6 months.

name: Iron Overload Screening
reasoning: Markedly elevated ferritin with high transferrin saturation raises concern for hereditary hemochromatosis or secondary iron overload.
recommendation: HFE gene testing, liver iron assessment (MRI), consider hepatology referral.

name: Severe Vitamin D Deficiency
reasoning: Vitamin D < 20 ng/mL indicates deficiency. Concurrent low calcium or elevated PTH suggests secondary hyperparathyroidism from vitamin D deficiency.
recommendation: High-dose vitamin D3 supplementation (50,000 IU weekly x 8 weeks), then maintenance 1000-2000 IU daily. Recheck in 3 months.
